question_title: Will Donald Trump fail to attend Liv Golf New York 2026?
summary: YES = Trump fails to attend LIV Golf New York at Bedminster (Aug 6-9, 2026). This resolves YES if the event is cancelled OR Trump is absent. The event is confirmed and considered LIV's most secure remaining event (Trump-Saudi ties), but LIV faces an acute funding crisis (PIF delivered only ~$200M of $600M, New Orleans already postponed, CEO won't guarantee any final-four event), giving ~18% cancellation risk. Conditional on the event running, Trump's attendance is near-certain (~92%): it's his own club, he attended LIV Virginia in May 2026 as a sitting president, and criteria only require him to be on the grounds during a four-day early-August window he routinely spends at Bedminster. P(NO)≈0.82×0.92≈0.75, so P(YES)≈0.25. The dominant driver of YES is event cancellation, not Trump's willingness.

factors.description: Event confirmed on LIV schedule, tickets on sale, considered 'most secure' of remaining events due to Trump-Saudi ties. Favors event happening → favors NO.
Acute LIV financial crisis: PIF delivered only $200M of $600M, New Orleans postponed, CEO won't guarantee any final-four event, WARN Act layoffs. Cancellation risk → favors YES.

Trump's consistent record of attending LIV at his own clubs incl. Virginia May 2026 as sitting president; Bedminster is his club, August peak season, lenient 'on grounds' criteria. Strongly favors NO.
Presidential schedule risk: travel abroad, crisis, or health could keep Trump away Aug 6-9 → favors YES.

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.25 (25%)** that Trump will fail to attend LIV Golf New York 2026.

The question resolves **NO** if Trump is on the Bedminster grounds while the event runs Aug 6-9. My decomposition:

- **P(event happens)** ≈ 82% — Confirmed on schedule and deemed LIV's "most secure" remaining event due to Trump-Saudi ties, but a genuine funding crisis (PIF delivered only ~$200M of $600M, New Orleans already postponed, CEO won't guarantee any final-four event) creates real cancellation risk.
- **P(Trump present | event runs)** ≈ 92% — Near-locked given his documented record (attended LIV Virginia in May 2026 as sitting president, played the 2022 Bedminster pro-am), his ownership of the club, peak August season, and lenient "on grounds" criteria.

P(NO) ≈ 0.75 → **P(YES) ≈ 0.25**. The dominant path to YES is event cancellation from LIV's financial collapse, not Trump declining to show.
resolution_criteria: This question resolves as **No** if, between August 6-9, 2026 (inclusive), President Donald Trump is anywhere within the grounds of the Trump National Golf Club Bedminster and [Liv Golf NY 2026](https://events.livgolf.com/new-york/) takes place there while Trump is present.

input.anchor: Conditional on event running, Trump attendance ~92% from documented record; event-happening ~82% given financial crisis but 'safest event' status.
input.assessment: YES = Trump fails to attend. Occurs if event cancelled (~18%, though Bedminster safest) OR event runs but Trump absent (~8% conditional). P(NO)=P(event)~0.82 × P(present|event)~0.92 ≈ 0.75 → P(YES)≈0.25. Factor sum with correct signs (~-1.48 logit → ~0.19) is slightly lower than my 0.25; I nudge up because cancellation and absence risks compound and financial-crisis tail is fatter than the point estimate. Dominant YES driver is event cancellation, not Trump's willingness.
input.key_uncertainties: Whether LIV financially delivers Bedminster; secondary presidential schedule conflicts.
input.tool_audit: research() returned well-sourced findings on both event status and Trump attendance history. No failures.
input.process_reflection: Clean two-component decomposition; research tool covered both. No friction.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
Verified all four factors against the research trace — every core claim is grounded, no hallucinations.

- Factor 1 (event confirmed, tickets on sale, "most secure" of remaining events due to Trump-Saudi ties): supported at trace lines 34-36, 44-45.
- Factor 2 (PIF delivered ~$200M of ~$600M, New Orleans postponed, CEO won't guarantee any final-four event, WARN Act layoffs): supported at lines 37-39, 48-58, with multiple corroborating sources (FT, ESPN, CBS, Front Office Sports, Sportico, CNN).
- Factor 3 (Trump attended LIV Virginia May 2026 as sitting president, played 2022 Bedminster pro-am, LIV at his courses every season): supported at lines 82-94, 1906.
- Factor 4 (presidential schedule risk): generic forward-looking prior, no fabrication.

Logit signs all correct and internally consistent: event-happening and Trump-attendance favor NO (negative); cancellation and schedule-conflict favor YES (positive). Notably the trace shows the agent detected and corrected an initial sign inconsistency (line 1936) — good hygiene.

No double-counting (factors 2 and 4 are distinct cancellation vs. personal-absence channels). Resolution criteria are provided and the agent decomposes exactly along them (event takes place AND Trump on grounds Aug 6-9). No pre-publication event issue — the resolution window is entirely after the July 12 publish date, and the forecast is built forward-looking.

Independent probability check: P(event runs) ~0.80-0.85 (safest event but genuine funding crisis), P(Trump present | runs) ~0.90-0.93 (documented record, own club, peak season, lenient "on grounds" criterion) → P(YES) ≈ 0.22-0.27. The agent's 0.25 sits squarely in my range. The modest nudge from factor-implied 0.195 up to 0.25 for compounding/fat-tail cancellation risk is reasoned, not a round-number hedge.

Counterargument is strong — it builds the coherent "Bedminster insulated, Trump attendance locked" alternative with specific evidence and a concrete 0.12-0.15 lower bound, and identifies the leading indicators (UK event Jul 23-26, PIF funding) that would move the estimate. Confidence 0.72 is well-calibrated given no confirmed Trump plan and a live financial crisis. Tool audit claim of "no failures" matches ground truth (0.0% error rate, 27 calls). No errors found.
